title: SOFI Stock Slides After Strong Earnings and 2026 Outlook
text: Despite posting solid Q1 2026 results, SoFi Technologies (NASDAQ: SOFI) experienced a sharp selloff, dropping 12–14.1% intraday to around $15.77–$16.16 after trading above $19 earlier in the week. The company delivered impressive operational results: revenue of $1.1 billion exceeded the $1.05 billion consensus, and adjusted EPS of $0.12 matched expectations. The report highlighted record adjusted net revenue and strong member growth. The decline stemmed from management's decision to maintain unchanged 2026 guidance rather than raise outlooks. The full-year targets include $4.655 billion in adjusted net revenue, $1.6 billion EBITDA, and $825 million in adjusted net income—all representing roughly 30% growth. Management projected Q2 2026 adjusted net revenue growth of approximately 30%, with EBITDA margins near 30% and net income margins of 12–13%. SOFI is expanding deeper into real estate lending through a fully digital Home Equity Line of Credit HELOC, a new Real Estate Advisory Council, expanded loan officer networks, and a guarantee to enhance competitiveness. The disconnect illustrates a classic market dynamic: strong fundamentals met investor disappointment over unchanged guidance, triggering a valuation reset in a stock that had already priced in higher expectations.
